After reviewing the pleadings and listening to oral arguments the Court rules as follows: DEFENDANT 1500 MISSION URBAN HOUSING, LLC, 1500 MISSION URBAN HOUSING LP's MOTION TO CONSOLIDATE ACTIONS OF CASE(S) CGC-20-587860, CGC-20-587017, CGC-20-585589, CGC-20-587274, CGC-20-587283, CGC-20-588485 WITH CASE CGC-20-585702 is GRANTED. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-14-2020 Continued To Mar-03-21 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

1. Notice Of Motion And Motion For The Following Orders: (1) To Order The Mechanics Lien Of Build Group, Inc. Be Reduced To $17,872,765.60; And (2) To Release The Subject Property From The Defective Mechanics Lien Of Van-Mulder Sheet Metal, Inc.
Off Calendar Dec-22-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Court Call):
Kevin Rooney, Esq. 415-421-3400 of Steyer Lowenthal Broodrookas Alvarez & Smith LLP for Plaintiff.
John Haushalter, Esq. 949-378-9436 of HGI Law Group for Defendant.

Defendant did not comply with the tentative ruling rule.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares its views and is not happy with the debate re: compliance with notice provisions. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT 1500 MISSION URBAN HOUSING, LLC, 1500 MISSION URBAN HOUSING LP 1. Notice Of Motion And Motion For The Following Orders: (1) To Order The Mechanics Lien Of Build Group, Inc. Be Reduced To $17,872,765.60; And (2) To Release The Subject Property From The Defective Mechanics Lien Of Van-Mulder Sheet Metal, Inc.

DENIED. Moving party failed to establish that Van-Mulder's lien is invalid and failed to cite authority for the proposition that Plaintiff's lien shall be reduced in any amount at this time.

Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. =(501/REQ)
